---
title: "Create map figure"
output: word_document
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

# Setup
```{r setup, include= F}
# specify data directory
data_dir <- Sys.getenv("data_dir")

# source setup file
source('setup.R')
```

# Open and prepare geocode data
```{r}
DF.geo <- 
  # open data
  readRDS(file.path(data_dir,
                    "location",
                    "btpsych24a_geocode.Rds"))

DF.geo <-
  
  # separate leader and supporter location information
  lapply(DF.geo,
         function(sublist)
           sublist %>% 
           mutate(
             leader_coun = .[['institution_country_code']][1],
             leader_lat = .[["latitude"]][1],
             leader_lon = .[["longitude"]][1],
             supporter_coun = .[['institution_country_code']],
             supporter_lat = .[["latitude"]],
             supporter_lon = .[["longitude"]]
           )
         )
```

## Identify first authors
```{r}
# prepare data
f.author <-
  # isolate first authors
  lapply(DF.geo,
         function(sublist)
           slice(sublist,
                 1)
         ) %>% 
  
  # bind rows
  bind_rows() %>% 
  
  # select country information
  select(leader_coun) %>% 
  rename(iso_a2 = leader_coun) %>% 
  
  # convert iso2 to iso3
  mutate(iso3 =
           countrycode(iso_a2, 
                       origin = "iso2c", 
                       destination = "iso3c")
         )

# export data
write.csv(x = f.author,
          file = file.path(data_dir,
                           'location',
                           'btpsych24a_f.author.csv'),
          row.names = F)
```

## Combine first author data with weird indicators
```{r}
# open weird data
DF.w <-
  readRDS(file.path(data_dir,
                    'worlddata',
                    'btpsych24a_weird.Rds')
          )
  

# for simplicity, calculate average 'ride' scores for each country
DF.w <- DF.w %>% 
  group_by(iso3) %>% 
  summarise(r = mean(r, na.rm = T),
            i = mean(i, na.rm = T),
            d = mean(d, na.rm = T),
            e = mean(e, na.rm = T))

# left join with f.author
f.author.w <- 
  left_join(f.author,
            DF.w,
            by = "iso3") %>% 
  
  # remove observations with no iso3 country code
  filter(!is.na(iso3))

rm(f.author, DF.geo)
```

# Create plots comparing population and sample distribution
Create general plotting function
```{r}
DataViz <- function(outcome,
                    label){
  ggplot(data = DF.w,
         aes(x = .data[[outcome]])) +
  
  # population distribution
  geom_density(color = 'purple4',
               adjust = 2) +
  
  # sample distribution
  geom_density(data = f.author.w,
               color = 'blue1',
               adjust = 5) +
  
  xlab(label) +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
        axis.title.y = element_blank())
}
```

Create figures
```{r}
r.fig <- DataViz(outcome = 'r',
                 label = 'GDP per capita (millions)')

i.fig <- DataViz(outcome = 'i',
                 label = 'Industrialization')

d.fig <- DataViz(outcome = 'd',
                 label = 'Democratic ratings')

e.fig <- DataViz(outcome = 'e',
                 label = 'Educational attainment')

plot_grid(r.fig,
          i.fig,
          d.fig,
          e.fig)
```
